textDisplay: Ex-machina, but a lot more…

I saw this movie during Mystery Movie Monday at my local theater, and I went in completely blind—it blew me away! I had no idea about any of the twists, which made the experience even more thrilling. My girlfriend thought we were walking into a romcom, so she was definitely surprised. Overall, an incredible movie and an unforgettable experience!
Saw the movie last night. I really enjoyed the movie. So go check it out

There's only one twist this trailer didn't reveal, but it happens shortly less than halfway through the movie so whatever. I'd never even heard of this movie going in so knew literally nothing, and I guess that goes for everyone else there because with every twist the audience gasped. So go in completely blind. However, it just occurred to me that if you're seeing this comment you've just seen the trailer so...yeah, oops.

I saw an early screening with the director. He said in a panel that the first teaser wasn’t generating enough buzz, so when it was time to make this trailer, when in his own words, “you’re competing with millions of other movies coming out on streaming,” you’ve no choice but to spoil some things. He REALLY didn’t want to throw in the robot twist, but he acknowledged that we don’t live in a world where you can go into a movie without knowing anything about it anymore.

I am happy I got to see this not knowing anything beyond who worked on it. The fantasy film fest ads for the movie litteraly were “the less you know about this the better” and they were right. 
In terms of them making money he might be right as well, but we can decide to be smarter. 😉
